observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
The Municipal Accountant to submit the Alpha list of the above remitted withholding taxes to facilitate verification; prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors; and record the withholding and remittances carefully to avoid misrepresentations in the financial statement;
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate remittance to BIR after due verification of the correct balances. Possible penalty and surcharges thereto shall not be charged against the LGU fund, such shall be personal liability of the withholding agents, as set forth in Revenue Regulation 2-98; and
The Municipal Treasurer to exclude in the payroll those employees opted to be paid by check for their salaries to avoid duplication of the recording of withholding of taxes and the unnecessary adjustments monthly.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant failed to submit the Alpha list of Payees pertaining to the CY 2023 remittances, and no adjusting entries were made to correct the recording errors. 

Reconciliation of records between the treasury and the accounting office were not initiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the Municipal Accountant to be mindful of the accounts used in the recording the fund transfer; (b) debit the Due to NGAs account only upon liquidation of the fund to the source agency; (c) recognized income equal to the amount deducted as liability for the non-capital asset expenditures; and (d) to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors noted.
observations.reason: Adjusting journal entries were not prepared by the Municipal Accountant to corrects the errors noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE, being the head of the procuring entity, agreed enjoin the Municipal Engineer to:

submit the variation orders issued by the procuring entity;

submit the notices of warning issued to the contractor; and

compute the total liquidated damages (TLD) and apply the same to the remaining balance due to the contractor, and if not enough to cover the TLD, demand immediate payment.

We further recommended that the LCE to:

ensure completeness of supporting documents and pass through the scrutiny of the Municipal Accountant prior to the approval for payment to contractor; and

refrain from paying the contractors in advance in which actual physical work accomplishments differ from the submitted SWA.
observations.reason:
Variation Orders remain unsubmitted. 

Notices of warning remain unsubmitted. 

Liquidated damages were not yet initiated by the procuring entity. 

There were infrastructure projects in which the contractor was already paid despite incomplete documentations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card based on the assessment roll submitted by the Municipal Assessor and furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as the basis for setting-up the RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer is still in the process of updating the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to update files in the MTO pertaining to the real property taxes, immediately send collection letters to the delinquent property owners and provide a copy of their payments to this Office, and prepare the Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of the Real Property Tax and post the same at the main entrance of the municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay of the local government unit concerned.
observations.reason: Copies of the collection letters to delinquent property owners were not submitted to the audit team, if any. Further, Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of the Real Property Tax was not prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to prepare the Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan in order to assess the required number of JO workers to be hired based on necessity and financial capability of the municipality.
observations.reason: The HRMO failed to submit the Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern and their assessment on the number of JOs to be hired based on necessity of all departments and financial capability of the municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to continue their collaborative efforts in pursuing the remaining and future LGSF- funded programs in accordance with the guidelines set by the DILG and submit the required documents to be prepared/submitted by them as mentioned in the guidelines.
 
Direct the BAC and its Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents as enumerated and warrant the completeness of supporting documents that need to be prepared/submitted in the procurement and implementation of the projects in accordance with DILG MCs and COA Circular 2012-001. 

Instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure completeness of supporting documents are attached to the DVs before making the payment to contractors.
observations.reason:
The MPDC and the Municipal Engineer failed to prepare and submit the status report on the implementation and completion of LGSF-funded programs. 

The personnel concerned failed to submit lacking documents. 

Based from the audit of payments to contractors with regards to infrastructure projects as of yearend, payments were made despite incomplete documentations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to order the physical count of inventories as of yearend and to prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of the following year.
observations.reason: The municipality failed to conduct the physical count of inventories, hence, the failure to prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE: 

require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare adjusting entries for the reversion of Watercrafts and Other Machinery and Equipment accounts; and

direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to, henceforth, strictly and efficiently observe compliance with the Chart of Accounts under GAM for LGUs with respect to recording of transactions in the books.
observations.reason: No preparation of the JEV to adjust recording errors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE to demand from the suppliers the issuance of sales invoice or official receipts, for submission to Commission on Audit (COA) for further evaluation.
observations.reason: The municipality failed to submit to the audit team the lacking sales invoice/ official receipts pertaining to the procurement of COVID-19 related goods and services.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE: 

ensure that extension of funds assistance to NGOs/POs be, henceforth, consistent with the prescribed guidelines under COA Circular No. 96-003; 

direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to recognize the funds assistance as Due from NGOs/POs in the books; and 

require the recipient-fisherfolks associations to submit status report on the utilization of the funds and the implementation of the projects.
observations.reason:
Fund assistance to NGOs/POs was not recorded as a receivable in the books of the municipality. 

Recipient barangay failed to submit the status report on the utilization of the funds and the implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and the management agreed to direct:
The Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entry to correct the recording errors; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct thorough financial investigation with regards to the occurrence of cash deficit and submit justification to the local chief executive, local sanggunian and to the audit team.

We recommended, and the Local School Board (LSB) agreed to:
Furnish copy of the approved SEF Budget to the members of the local finance committee not later 7 days from its approval;
Ensure that all funds available including the prior year’s unexpended balance be included in the SEF Budget and provided with responsive and necessary PPAs, and ensure that all expenditures are consistent with the approved budget; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the accuracy of the quarterly utilization reports to be uploaded in the DILG FDPP website, duly validated and reconciled with the records of the Municipal Budget Office, and copy furnished the LSB Secretary and the audit team.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to (a) require all collectors to prepare and submit on time the correct form for RAAF, as well as the CRAAF and validate the ending balances reported with the actual unused accountable forms; (b) submit all obsolete bank checks accompanied by inventory reports; (c) ensure that all collections made are duly acknowledged by issuing the appropriate accountable forms and maintain a cashbook for recording purposes; and (d) impose internal control measures for the immediate remittance of collections.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct: 

the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period duly supported with the required documents, and   ensure that receipt of the report is properly acknowledged by the receiving Office;

the Municipal Accountant to verify the reports submitted by the Municipal Treasurer, communicate all the lacking documents, record in the books and submit the same with all the vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents to the Auditor within 10 days after receipt thereof.  Most importantly, ensure that a cash advance will not be granted unless the previous cash advances are fully liquidated, and attach a certification signed by the Accountant that all previous cash advances are fully liquidated and accounted for before granting any additional cash advance; and

initiate regular reconciliation of records between the Treasurer’s Office and the Accounting Office, and make the necessary adjustments/journal entries, if warranted.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer to monitor and strictly follow the provisions on the posting of signboards in order to promote good governance through transparency and accountability; to encourage public participation therein; and to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern. 

Moving forward, submit photographs of the tarpaulin signboard posted in the project location for future infrastructure projects of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to always see to it that remittances to BIR be supported with Monthly Alphalist of Payees (MAP), correct and required tax return and remit all the taxes on or before the due date to avoid possible imposition of penalty/surcharge.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to enjoin:

the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from preparing the checks for payment wanting the certification in the DVs of the Municipal Accountant as to the completeness of supporting documents; and 

the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents, and to strictly observe the timely submission of accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-006 PD No. 1445.
We recommended and the LCE, being the head of the agency, agreed to spearhead the preparation and the submission of the AAPSI CY 2022 and prior years with much urgency.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers/Records for the balances of the controlling account appearing in the General Ledger and provide for the details of the non-moving/dormant accounts.
observations.reason: Implemented

observations.recommendation:
1.26 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant to:
i. prepare JEV to re-classify the projects that are still on-going;
ii. prepare JEV to correct the misclassification of PPE to another account;
iii. prepare the JEV to correct the erroneous recognition and non-
recognition of PPE account;
iv. ensure that depreciation is properly provided for each class of PPE as
discussed in PAG3 of the IPSAS 17 and provide for the detailed schedule
of depreciation;
v. prepare and maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledgers of
each PPE account;
b. The General Service Officer to prepare and maintain property cards for all
PPEs of the Municipality and effect regular reconciliation with the ledger cards
of the Accounting Office; and
c. The Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the approved Physical
Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the
scheduled start of inventory - taking activities, conduct and complete the
physical inventory in three months or less and ensure full implementation of
“One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular No.
2020-006.

2.18 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
a. The Municipal Accountant to submit the Alpha list of the above remitted
withholding taxes to facilitate verification; prepare the necessary adjusting
entries to correct the errors; and record the withholding and remittances
carefully to avoid misrepresentations in the financial statement;
b. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to cause the
immediate remittance to BIR after due verification of the correct balances.
Possible penalty and surcharges thereto shall not be charged against the
LGU fund, such shall be personal liability of the withholding agents, as set
forth in Revenue Regulation 2-98; and
c. The Municipal Treasurer to exclude in the payroll those employees opted to be
paid by check for their salaries to avoid duplication of the recording of
withholding of taxes and the unnecessary adjustments monthly.

3.22 We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the Municipal
Accountant to be mindful of the accounts used in the recording the fund
transfer; (b) debit the Due to NGAs account only upon liquidation of the fund
to the source agency; (c) recognized income equal to the amount deducted as
liability for the non-capital asset expenditures; and (d) to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors noted.

4.22 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
a. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to initiate
the reconciliation of records covering the periods CY 2019 to CY 2023 and
should arrive at the correct balance of the unspent portion of the DRRM
Fund and moving forward, to implement regular reconciliation
particularly at year end;
b. The Municipal Accountant to:
i. Coordinate with the former Municipal Accountant to reconcile
records of the amount recorded and charged to the Special Trust
Fund- DRRM especially those that were utilized during the
pandemic;
ii. Draw an adjusting journal entry to record the correct balance to the
Trust Liabilities- Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
account;
iii. Maintain and update the related subsidiary ledgers;
c. The Municipal Treasurer to:
i. transfer to the DRRM Fund bank account the erroneous deposit in
the Trust Fund bank account in CYs 2019, 2021 and 2022 after the
reconciliation of records between the former Accountant and the
Municipal Budget Officer; and
ii. facilitate the actual transfer of the unspent CY 2023 DRRM fund to
its proper bank account.

5.13 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
a. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to verify and
ensure that all programs and projects implemented financed by the FY
2022 LGSF-GEF are incorporated in the DTP and AIP;
b. The Local Finance Committee composed of the Local Budget Officer,
Local Treasurer, Local Planning and Development Coordinator to
prepare and submit the Report on Fund Utilization and Status of
Program/Project Implementation;
c. The Municipal Treasurer to verify the accuracy of the undisbursed
balance and consequently issue payment to revert the undisbursed
balance to the National Treasury; and
d. The Municipal Accountant to submit the DVs with complete supporting
documents and exercise prudence in allowing the payment to contractors
despite incomplete documents.

6.22 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
a. The Municipal Engineer to fast track the completion/acquisition of the
development projects, and moving forward, to ensure that all development
projects are completed as of yearend;
b. The BAC Chairperson to ensure completeness of mandatory documents
prior to the perfection of the contract;
c. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all DVs and supporting documents
passed meticulous scrutiny prior to its payment, and initiate the immediate
submission of lacking DVs together with complete supporting documents;
d. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure that only
those infrastructure projects that are necessary and appropriate should be
included under the 20% DF; and
e. The Municipal Accountant to submit the corresponding MOA for the
KALAHI CIDSS related projects.

7.21 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
a. The LDRRM Officer to prepare LDRRMFIP for the current year’s
appropriations and the unexpended DRRMF using the prescribed format
in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002, and in the preparation of the
LDRRMFIP for the unexpended balance of the DRRMF, budget for each
PPA should be specified as to the year of unutilized balance it will be
charged and not in lumpsum;
b. The Municipal Accountant to conduct thorough verification on the
unexpended balances of DRRMF for CYs 2015, 2016, and 2017 if said
unexpended balances remain unutilized, and if warranted and
supported with actual cash, be transferred to the General Fund for
social services PPAs;
c. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the correct LDRRMF Utilization
Report duly validated and reconciled with the records of the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Budget Officer and
be submitted to the audit team within the reglementary period, and ensure
to transfer the correct unutilized balance of the QRF and MOOE to the
STF, adjusted based on the actual income realized;
d. The Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from including unexpended QRF
and DRRM-MOOE as part of the Continuing Appropriation;
e. The Municipal Accountant to initiate the immediate submission of the
identified disbursement vouchers and its complete supporting documents,
and practice utmost judiciousness in identifying eligible expenses to be
charged against the DRRMF particularly those administrative expenses
and may constitutes unnecessary expenses; and
f. The Municipal Treasurer to refund the service charge debited to the DRRM
Fund.

8.12 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to (a)
require all collectors to prepare and submit on time the correct form for RAAF,
as well as the CRAAF and validate the ending balances reported with the
actual unused accountable forms; (b) submit all obsolete bank checks
accompanied by inventory reports; (c) ensure that all collections made are
duly acknowledged by issuing the appropriate accountable forms and
maintain a cashbook for recording purposes; and (d) impose internal control
measures for the immediate remittance of collections.

9.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
a. the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant the
liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period duly
supported with the required documents, and ensure that receipt of the
report is properly acknowledged by the receiving Office;
b. the Municipal Accountant to verify the reports submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer, communicate all the lacking documents, record in the books
and submit the same with all the vouchers/payrolls and supporting
documents to the Auditor within 10 days after receipt thereof. Most
importantly, ensure that a cash advance will not be granted unless the
previous cash advances are fully liquidated, and attach a certification
signed by the Accountant that all previous cash advances are fully
liquidated and accounted for before granting any additional cash advance;
and
c. initiate regular reconciliation of records between the Treasurer’s Office
and the Accounting Office, and make the necessary adjustments/journal
entries, if warranted.

10.13 We recommended and the LCE, being the head of the procuring entity, agreed to
enjoin the Municipal Engineer to:
a. Submit the variation orders issued by the procuring entity;
b. Submit the notices of warning issued to the contractor; and
c. Compute the total liquidated damages (TLD) and apply the same to the
remaining balance due to the contractor, and if not enough to cover the TLD,
demand immediate payment.

10.14 We further recommended that the LCE to:
a. Initiate procedures in the blacklisting of the contractor concerned to participate
in the bidding of government projects/contracts on the ground of negative
slippage of 15% and above within the critical path of the project due entirely
97
to the fault or negligence of the contractor;
b. Ensure completeness of supporting documents and pass through the scrutiny of
the Municipal Accountant prior to the approval for payment to contractor; and
c. Refrain from paying the contractors in advance in which actual physical work
accomplishments differ from the submitted SWA.

11.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer to
monitor and strictly follow the provisions on the posting of signboards in order to
promote good governance through transparency and accountability; to
encourage public participation therein; and to secure the right of the people to
information on matters of public concern.

11.8 Moving forward, submit photographs of the tarpaulin signboard posted in the
project location for future infrastructure projects of the Municipality.

12.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to
always see to it that remittances to BIR be supported with Monthly Alphalist
of Payees (MAP), correct and required tax return and remit all the taxes on or
before the due date to avoid possible imposition of penalty/surcharge.

13.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to enjoin:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to refrain from preparing the checks for payment
wanting the certification in the DVs of the Municipal Accountant as to the
completeness of supporting documents; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement
vouchers with complete supporting documents, and to strictly observe the
timely submission of accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2009-
006 PD No. 1445.

14.6 We recommended and the LCE, being the head of the agency, agreed to
spearhead the preparation and the submission of the AAPSI CY 2022 and
prior years with much urgency.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to continue their collaborative efforts in pursuing the remaining and future LGSF-funded programs in accordance with the guidelines set by the DILG and submit the required documents to be prepared/submitted by them as mentioned in the guidelines. b. Direct the BAC and its Secretariat to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents as enumerated and as evidenced by its warrant the failure to submit the completeness of supporting documents that need to be prepared/submitted in the procurement and implementation of the projects in accordance with DILG MCs and COA Circular 2012-001. c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure completeness of supporting documents are attached to the DVs before making the payment to contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with the requirement of submission of contracts within five days from execution under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, and of sending invitation to observers pursuant to 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the LCE: a. ensure that extension of funds assistance to NGOs/POs be, henceforth, consistent with the prescribed guidelines under COA Circular No. 96-003; b. direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to recognize the funds assistance as Due from NGOs/POs in the books; and c. require the recipient-fisherfolks associations to submit status report on the utilization of funds and the implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers/Records for the balances of the controlling account appearing in the General Ledger and provide for the details of non-moving/dormant accounts.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledgers/records were not prepared and maintained due to insufficient manpower in the accounting unit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require:
a. all the RCCs and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the monthly RAAF
and the Consolidated RAAF, respectively to facilitate proper monitoring
of receipts and issuances of accountable forms;
b. the Municipal Treasurer to established proper cut-off for the remittances
collected at year-end, as well as of CTC collections by the barangay and
submit immediately to the Municipal Accountant the RCDs for recording;
and
c. the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
correct the errors and unreported collections.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to immediately update the Real Property Tax
Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card based on the assessment roll
submitted by the Municipal Assessor and furnish the Municipal
Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the
amount due and collectible for the year as the basis for setting-up the RPT
and SET Receivables; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that (i) the correct basis is used to
book up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year, (ii)
properly record the transactions pertaining to the collections of property
taxes, and (iii) prepare adjusting entries in order to rectify the noted
variances of the affected accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
1. require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare JEV to:
i. re-classify the projects that are still on-going and not yet
delivered;
ii. to correct the misclassification of PPE to another account;
iii. to correct the erroneous recognition and non-recognition of PPE
account;
b. ensure that depreciation is properly provided for each class of PPE as
discussed in PAG3 of the IPSAS 17 and provide for the detailed
schedule of depreciation;
c. disclosed in the Notes to FS the correct amounts to support the data
presented the Financial Statements; and
d. prepare and maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledgers of each
PPE account.
2. require the General Service Officer to prepare and maintain property
cards for all PPEs of the Municipality and effect regular reconciliation
with the ledger cards of the Accounting Office; and
3. require the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the approved Physical
Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the
scheduled start of inventory- taking activities, conduct and complete the
physical inventory in three months or less and ensure full implementation of
“One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular No.
2020-006.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to
refrain from recording Accounts Payable based only on the issued Obligation
Request, but rather on an obligation that has become due and demandable;
and make the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the
affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
a. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to initiate the
reconciliation of records covering the periods CY 2019 to CY 2022 and
should arrive at the correct balance of the unspent portion of the DRRM
Fund and moving forward, to implement regular reconciliation
particularly at year end;
b. the Municipal Accountant to:
i. Draw an adjusting journal entry to record the correct balance to the Trust
Liabilities- Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
account;
ii. Maintain and update the related subsidiary ledgers;
c. the Municipal Treasurer to:
i. transfer to the DRRM Fund bank account the erroneous deposit in the
Trust Fund bank account of ₱1,003,692.31 in CY 2022 and
₱3,289,162.52 in CY 2021; and
ii. facilitate the actual transfer of the unspent CY 2022 and CY 2021
continuing DRRM fund to its proper bank account.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the:
a. Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal
Planning Development Coordinator to:
i. plan the procurement and implementation of the identified priority
development projects under the 20% DF to ensure the completion
of the PPAs;
ii. confirm the feasibility of the PPAs and observe timeliness in their
implementation to achieve the desired socio-economic development
targets and outcomes;
b. Municipal Budget Officer to incorporate in the SAAOB the changes of
PPAs during the year, refrain from charging/obligating the total contract
price when paying for the 15% mobilization and obligation shall be
reflected of the actual amount to be utilized;
c. Municipal Engineer to submit the list of completed projects to the Municipal
Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to facilitate the reversion and re-
appropriation of savings/unexpended balances from completed projects
under the 20% DF to eligible development projects and follow strict compliance on
the implementation period as indicated in the AIP;
d. Municipal Engineer and the BAC to provide periodically the Municipal Budget
Officer and the Municipal Accountant with the status of the projects and
procurements undertaken by the Municipality to assist the concerned offices in
period review and monitoring of balances of current and continuing
appropriations;
e. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and submit
accurate and correct report to oversight agencies for proper monitoring.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to ensure that Acknowledgment
Receipt be issued for the receipt of In-Kind Donations and require the
following:
a. the General Services Officer- Designate, and the Municipal Health
Officer to coordinate in the preparation of the monthly reports on the
receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of donated items and
account for the unutilized items at year end, if any and prepare and
maintain a Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM;
b. the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary disclosure in the Notes
to FS as to the quantity of received, issued and balance of all donation-
in-kind received by the Municipality, with categorization as to food
items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and others; and
c. the Municipal Health Officer to provide a written justification on the
receipt of expired vaccines.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to provide valid written justification
on allowing the contractor to be paid despite doubtful physical
accomplishment, and to require:
a. The Inspectorate Team to actually conduct its inspection per the
submission of the SWA/ Progress Billing by the contractor and ensure that
payment thereof is equivalent to the contractor’s work accomplished and
a representative of the actual and physical accomplishment of the project;
and
b. the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all supporting documents are
available, submitted, and should be reviewed and evaluated before
certifying the disbursement voucher as to its completeness.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to
update files in the MTO pertaining to the real property taxes, immediately
send collection letters to the delinquent property owners and provide a copy
of their payments to this Office, and prepare the Notice of Delinquency in the
Payment of the Real Property Tax and post the same at the main entrance of
the municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each
barangay of the local government unit concerned;

Require the officials and employees of the Municipality to make prompt
payment of their real property tax dues; and

We likewise recommended that the legislative officials, in consultation with the
Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to draw up plans to
strategize and strengthen the collection drive beyond the required posting and
sending of notices.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Human
Resource Management Officer to:
a. prepare the Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan in order
to assess the required number of JO workers to be hired based on
necessity and financial capability of the municipality;
b. prepare a valid Job Order contract of service or Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) paid on a daily basis and period should not exceed 6
months; and
c. adopt a monitoring mechanism in order to avoid double payment of
services rendered and ensure that accomplishments be meticulously
evaluated and assessed prior to the renewal of JO contract.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
a. the LDRRM Officer prepare the DRRM Budget consistent with the
DRRM Plan approved and adopted by the MDRRM Council and Local
Sanggunian, including all the unexpended prior years balances in its
LDRRMIP using the format specified in Annex A of COA Circular No.
2012-002;
b. the Municipal Accountant to properly prepare the LDRRMF Utilization
Report duly supported with details of expenditures and submit the same
audit team within the prescribed period;
c. the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registries of RAAO for
the QRF, MOOE, and Capital Outlay and refrain from including

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (37) strictly enforce the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations, Section 4(6) of PD 1445, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: Several procurement in CY 2018, though small in amounts, were made on reimbursement basis. Complete documentation of procurement transactions were not likewise strictly observed, as per Findings No. 10 and 11, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to draw the necessary correcting entries for the proper adjustment of
CIB-LCCA, taking into account the unknown reconciling items and net
understatement of the account by P1,358,091.85, and restoration of the amount or
value of stale checks to the agency’s CIB-LCCA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to properly recognize depreciation on property, plant and equipment
in accordance with PPSAS No. 17 and to maintain property, plant and equipment
ledger cards, and instruct the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer to
maintain property cards. We likewise recommend the constitution of the inventory
committee to conduct physical count of all PPE and render a report thereon, in
accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and the Handbook on
Property and Supply Management.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers/Records for the balances
of the controlling account appearing in the General Ledger and provide for the
details of the non-moving/dormant accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive oblige the Budget Officer to
refrain from allocating and utilizing portion of the 20% DF for non-related
programs/projects/activities. We likewise recommend the full implementation of
programmed projects and activities and the timely submission of report on the
utilization of current and continuing appropriation of 20% DF and henceforth,
strictly observe the provisions set forth under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated
February 22, 2017.

We recommended that Management ensure that no less than 5% of the estimated
annual revenues be appropriated for LDRRMF and to refrain from utilizing
portions of the fund for non-disaster-related programs, projects and activities. We
also recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the strict observance of the
accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF as enunciated under COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict compliance with
Section 4(6) of PD 1445 on the proper documentation of payment of wages and
advise the Sangguniang Bayan on the proper augmentation of funds as provided
under Section 336 of RA 7160.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Point System
(GFPS) to fully comply with the preparation and submission of GAD
accomplishment report as enunciated under the Joint Memorandum Circular Nos.
16-01 and 13-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to submit the
perfected contracts and their supporting documents within five (5) days from
execution thereof and to rectify, if possible, all deficiencies noted in the conduct of
legal and auditorial review of contracts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to strictly
comply with the provisions set forth under RA 9184 and its IRR on the invitation
of observers and posting requirement of Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and
the approved contract.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and other concerned agency officials to rectify all deficiencies noted
and henceforth, to ensure compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on complete documentation of
infrastructure transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to strictly
comply with the related provisions and procedures enunciated under RA 9184 and
its Revised IRR in the conduct of shopping and small value procurement on
procurement of goods. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Accountant be
directed to substantiate all payments for delivered goods and henceforth, to ensure
compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009 on complete documentation of procurement transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer to submit the lacking eleven (11) paid disbursement
vouchers and seventy (70) checks reported as cancelled to COA for audit and
verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (1) prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement for all funds pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to (6) strictly observe the provisions of Item 4.0 of the DEPED, DBM and DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned officials of the municipality to (16) strictly follow effective monthly rates of representation and transportation allowances (RATA) pursuant to Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013. We also recommended to (17) refund the amount ₱247,584.00 representing excess amount collected in CY 2017.
observations.reason: No refund of the amount representing excessive grant of RATA was made yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive discontinue the payment of retainer’s fees without the required written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General and the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant (19) submit immediately the one hundred seven (107) Disbursement Vouchers together with the supporting documents which were not attached to the submitted Report of Checks Issued and (20) strictly observe the timely submission of the accounts in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant (19) submit immediately the accounts to the Auditor, copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance within sixty days from receipt of CY 2017 AAR.
observations.reason: Several disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents remained unsubmitted as of year-end. Please see Finding No. 12, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (21) submit an explanation why the requisition of the 1,550 bags of cement was split into ten Purchase Requests. Likewise, (22) submit an inspection and acceptance report and list of recipients of the procured bags of cement, as well as a properly filled up Purchase Request, and Purchase Order. Likewise (23) stop the practice of splitting requisitions, as this is strictly prohibited by law.
observations.reason: No explanation was submitted yet. Required documents remain unsubmitted to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management (24) limit the use of LDRRMF for purposes that are outlined in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 and (25) require the Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Some utilizations of the CY 2018 LDRRMF pertain to non-disaster-related expenditures.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to come up with correct balance considering the non-availability of subsidiary ledgers of the Former Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: (33) Reconciliation of records is still on-going to determine the correct balance considering the non-availability of subsidiary ledgers of the Former Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (34) strictly implement the provisions of Section 66, Chapter 10, Title 1, Book V of Executive Order No. 292 s. 1987 and Section 37 of the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2013, thus employees who availed loans shall personally settle their obligations directly with these private lending institutions.

We recommended that the Revenue Collection Clerks be required to turnover their collections to the Liquidating Officer as mandated by Section 307 of RA 7160 and Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant immediately submit the accounts and to strictly observe the timely submission of the same in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The LGU Officials promised to confer with the spouse of the former Municipal Accountant and submit the one hundred seven (107) Disbursement Vouchers together with the supporting documents which were not attached to the submitted Report of Checks Issued.
observations.reason: At the time the former Municipal Accountant prepared the Year End Financial Statements in 2015, he died. The documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor and the concerned officials formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit the duly accomplished AAPSI report to the Auditor, copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance within sixty (60) days from receipt of the AAR in compliance with law and COA regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to submit the AAPSI for CY 2016 within 60 days from its receipt.
observations.reason: The Audit Team did not receive the AAPSI for CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly implement the provisions of Section 66, Chapter 10, Title I, Book V of Executive Order No. 292 s. 1987 and Section 37 of RA 10352 to ensure that employees only avail of loans that will not reduce their take home pay below the minimum prescribed.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to implement the required measures and the affected employees shall personally settle their obligations directly with these private institutions.
observations.reason: No action taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 and require Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travel and Driver's Trip Tickets be submitted.
observations.managementAction: Management promised that Daily Trip Tickets & Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption will be required in transactions.
observations.reason: No action taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly enforce the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised IRR and ensure procurements are done through the regular procurement process and that necessary documentary support accompany claims.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to strictly enforce the procurement rules and secure supporting documents.
observations.reason: No action taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Revenue Collection Clerks be required to turnover their collections to the Liquidating Officer as mandated by Section 307 of RA 7160 and Section 26 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer promised to devise strategies to require RCCs for the immediate turnover of collections.
observations.reason: No action taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement for all funds
pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and Sections 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4 of COA Circular
No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Budget
Officer, Municipal Accountant, MDRR Focal Person, and other concerned
officials to (a) provide a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP for the unexpended
previous years’ balance of the LDRRMF; (b) render a monthly Report on the
Sources and Utilization of the fund in the prescribed format; (c) transfer the
unexpended balance of LDRRMF to a Special Trust Fund; and (d) disclose in
the Notes to Financial Statement the details and amount available and utilized
during the year and the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to strictly
observe the provisions of Item 4.0 of the DEPED, DBM and DILG Joint
Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local
Finance Committee to include in the Annual Budget an appropriation
equivalent to 1% of IRA to finance Local Council for the Protection of Children
activities, cause the creation of LCPC and adopt a comprehensive plan and
program for the welfare of the children.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Officer, Municipal Engineer and other officials of
the municipality to fully utilize and implement the 20% development projects in
accordance with the mandate of the Local Government Code and DILG/DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should create and
organize an Internal Audit Services unit for the municipality in compliance with
R.A. No 4177 and AO 70 to give assurance of a sound internal control system in
the operations of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the 55% PS
limitation required in Section 325(a) of Republic Act 7160 and implement a
compensation schedule based on the income classification of a 5th class
municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and
Awards Chairman and Secretary to submit all the documents forming part of
the contract within five (5) days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No.
87-278 dated November 12, 1987, No. 89-299 dated March 21, 1989 and No.
2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and other Division Heads concerned in the discharge of financial
transactions to strictly observe Section 4(6) of the PD 1445 by ensuring that the
claims against government funds are supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned
officials of the municipality to strictly follow effective CY 2018 the correct
monthly rates of representation and transportation allowances (RATA)
pursuant to Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013. We also
recommended to refund the amount ₱247,584.00 representing excess amount
collected in CY 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to discontinue the payment of retainer’s fees without the required
written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General and the written
concurrence of the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare and submit a Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and to strictly observe the timely submission of the AAR and supporting schedules on schedule, as provided for in COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 10, 2010. To forestall repetition of the same, the Local Chief Executive should cause the imposition of the administrative sanction provided for in Section 26, Chapter 4 of the Executive Order No. 292 or the Administrative Code of 1987.
observations.managementAction: The new Municipal Accountant assumed office in February 2018 and he was able to submit the CY 2017 Financial Statements and supporting schedules on March 2, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the 20% Development Fund be strictly utilized for those that are of the LGU development and outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 Budget Officer and other officials of the LGU promised to strictly follow the said regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the officials/employees concerned be directed to liquidate their outstanding cash advances pursuant to the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Liquidations of Cash Advances were strictly monitored.
observations.reason: Hence, a minimal amount of unliquidated balance at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 and require the Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 recommended the Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRM Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management analyze the causes for, and clear out the negative balances from the books of accounts and the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management promised to investigate the causes for and clear out the negative balances from the books of accounts and the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide a budget for GAD equivalent to at least 5% of the municipality's annual budget in accordance with Section 34 of the General Appropriation Act, and ensure GAD expenditures clearly address gender issues.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, the 5% GAD was already included in the LGU’s CY 2012 budget. AAR recommended compliance.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to strictly observe the timely submission of the DVs, payrolls, ORs and other financial reports, in accordance with Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that management provide a budget for GAD equivalent to at least 5% of the municipality’s total appropriation in accordance with Section 34 of the General Appropriations Act of 2015 and Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 of DBM, NEDA and NCRFW. Likewise, limit the use of the GAD Budget to expenditures that address gender issues.

We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 with regard to appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund for development projects to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowances resulting from the post-audit of transactions.

We recommend that the LGU strictly observe the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 and its applicable IRR, especially on the prohibition on splitting of contracts in its procurement transactions. We further recommend the immediate submission of the lacking documents noted in the sampled transactions.

We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations, and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 93 of RA 10651.

We recommend that management through the Municipal Mayor ensure substantial compliance with the procurement process under the alternative methods of procurement.

We recommend that management limit the use of the LDRRMF to activities that support disaster risk management or for purposes that are outlined in DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012 and COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

report and list of recipients of the procured bags of cement, as well as a

immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances pursuant to the
